markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Taipei be 27°C on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Taipei Songshan Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Taipei Songshan Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/tw/taipei/RCSS.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Taiwan's Central Weather Administration (CWA) forecasts a high near 27°C for Taipei on May 13, driven by a persistent Pacific high-pressure ridge fostering stable, sunny conditions with subsidence warming aloft. This aligns with global model consensus from ECMWF and GFS ensembles, following May 10's observed 29°C peak—the warmest recent reading—while earlier May days averaged 22-26°C amid passing fronts. May climatology typically delivers highs of 27-29°C with moderate humidity around 80%, low precipitation risk, and influences from urban heat island effects at the official Songshan station. Inherent short-range forecast uncertainty persists; monitor CWA's twice-daily updates for refinements ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Will the Communist Party of the Russian Federation (KPRF) win the most seats in the next Russian parliamentary election?
markets.description:
Parliamentary elections are to be scheduled to be held in Russia in September 2026.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Russian State Duma election.

If the results are not known definitively by September 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

In the event of a tie between multiple parties for the most seats won, this market will resolve in favor of the party that received a greater number of valid votes. In the event that results in a tie, this market will resolve in favor of the party whose listed abbreviation appears first in alphabetical order.

This market's resolution will be based solely on the number of seats won by the named party in the State Duma of the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results reported by Russian government sources such as the Central Election Commission of the Russian Federation.

Note: If a listed party contests the election as part of a jointly listed coalition with one or more parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the listed party will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If two or more listed parties contest the election as a jointly listed coalition, with or without additional parties not listed in this market, the market corresponding to the party within that coalition that won the most seats in the previous election will represent the coalition and will count all seats won by the coalition collectively. If such a coalition dissolves, each market will again represent its respective party individually.

Late April polls (FOM, WCIOM) show ER leading at 35–52% versus 10–16% for nearest rivals like LDPR or New People, bolstered by January's electronic primaries and recent campaign guidance addressing voter concerns amid economic strains. Systemic opposition parties face internal crises and low regional traction, limiting challenges. Scenarios altering this include acute economic collapse sparking unrest, elite fractures, or exposed electoral irregularities, though Kremlin crackdowns and unity pledges reduce likelihood.

markets.question: Will Racing Bulls get pole position at the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix?

If the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix is canceled or rescheduled to a date after May 30, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve in favor of the constructor team that is officially recognized by Formula 1 as having set the fastest time during the qualifying session for the 2026 F1 Canadian Grand Prix. The market will be settled based on the FIA's official qualifying results, regardless of any subsequent penalties, disqualifications, or changes to the starting grid.

For example, if a constructor team sets the fastest qualifying time but later receives a grid penalty or is moved down the starting order, the market will still resolve to “Yes” for that constructor team.

The resolution source will be the official Formula 1 website and a consensus of credible sports news reporting.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping visit US before 2027?
markets.description:

For the purpose of this market, a "visit" is defined as Xi Jinping physically entering the terrestrial or maritime territory of the United States. Whether or not Xi Jinping enters US airspace during the timeframe of this market will have no bearing on a positive resolution.

The primary resolution source for this information will be official information from the US or China, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Donald Trump announce Todd Blanche as the next United States Attorney General by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the first individual Donald Trump announces as his pick to be United States Attorney General. 

An announcement from Donald Trump or the Trump administration stating their intent to nominate a specific individual for United States Attorney General will suffice to resolve this market, regardless of whether a formal nomination actually occurs.

Qualifying announcements must explicitly present the relevant individual as the nominee or future nominee for Attorney General. Announcements of acting or interim appointments, or announcements which merely reveal potential candidates, will not qualify. Media reports, speculation, or other unofficial information will not qualify.

A formal presidential nomination of an individual to be United States Attorney General will also suffice to resolve this market. Formal nominations are defined as the submission of a nomination message to the U.S. Senate.

If Donald Trump does not announce his pick for the next United States Attorney General by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No announcement by June 30".

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Donald Trump and the Trump Administration; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Top Goalscorer
events.description:
This market will resolve to the player who scores the most goals through all main tournament rounds of the 2026 FIFA World Cup competition.

In the event of a tie, this market will resolve according to the official leader as determined by FIFA rules. If multiple leaders are announced then this market will resolve to the player who scored fewer goals from penalty kicks. If a tie still persists, this market will resolve to the player whose listed last name comes first alphabetically.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there is no leader declared within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “Other”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kylian Mbappé holds a slim edge in trader consensus for the 2026 FIFA World Cup Golden Boot at 16.5% implied probability, fueled by his explosive 24-goal La Liga campaign with Real Madrid this season and consistent France national team strikes during qualifiers, while Harry Kane trails closely at 14.5% on his clinical finishing—highlighted by braces in England's perfect 8-0 qualifying record and recent international prep. The tight race reflects a crowded field of elite forwards from powerhouse nations like France, England, and Spain, with Erling Haaland fading to 7.5% amid spring knee injuries and missed Manchester City matches, and Mikel Oyarzabal surging to 6.5% after 11 goals in Spain's last 10 caps. Breakout threats like Lamine Yamal and Michael Olise add volatility as final friendlies and squad announcements loom a month out.

markets.question: Will Chelsea Clinton be the democratic nominee for NY-12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Democratic Party to contest the NY-12 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Democratic primary will take place on June 23, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democrat sources, including https://democrats.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the closely contested NY-12 Democratic primary on June 23 to replace retiring Rep. Jerry Nadler, trader consensus favors Assemblymember Alex Bores at 46.5% implied probability, driven by a surge in super PAC support—including $3.5 million from crypto billionaire Chris Larsen and $1.9 million from pro-AI regulation group You Can Push Back—plus the United Federation of Teachers endorsement last week and his campaign's first TV ad launch. Assemblymember Micah Lasher holds 32.5% amid Gov. Kathy Hochul's April endorsement and prior Bloomberg backing, though his charter school ties drew union opposition. Jack Schlossberg trails at 15.5% on Kennedy family name recognition and early polling leads, but lacks major institutional support like the Working Families Party. Recent analyses highlight older voters (50+) comprising up to 75% of the electorate as a pivotal bloc ahead of early voting.

markets.question: Will Mike Kennealy win the 2026 Massachusetts Governor Republican primary election?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement of the results from the Massachusetts Republican Party; however, an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

events.title: Massachusetts Governor Republican Primary Winner
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Republican Primary for Governor of Massachusetts, scheduled to take place on September 1, 2026. Resolution will be based on the overall winner of the primary, including any potential second round or run-off.

If no 2026 Massachusetts Gubernatorial Republican Primary takes place, this market will resolve to “Other.”

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael Minogue's commanding 70% victory at the Massachusetts Republican Party convention on April 26 secured the official endorsement and narrowed the gubernatorial primary field to him and Brian Shortsleeve, propelling trader consensus to price Minogue at 85% implied probability for the September primary win. This grassroots delegate support, combined with Minogue's self-funding as a biotech executive and combat veteran, has fueled a post-convention pressure campaign urging Shortsleeve to exit, elevating his odds to 16.7% amid resistance from the Marine veteran challenger. Former Housing Secretary Mike Kennealy's 1.1% standing reflects his failure to advance, leaving primary voters as the decider in this low-turnout contest.

markets.question: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: U.S. enacts AI safety bill before 2027?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a bill that includes at least one of the following provisions is signed into federal law in the United States  by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

- Prohibition on Creation or Release: Forbids the creation or release of specific AI systems or models.

- Training Restrictions: Sets limits on how AI systems can be trained, such as restricting access to previously available training data or imposing a maximum limit on the number of parameters used for training.

- Usage Restrictions:  Prevents AI systems from being used in certain applications, such as interacting with customers, interfacing with other applications, or performing actions on the web.

- Human-in-the-Loop Requirements: Requires AI systems to include mechanisms ensuring human oversight or involvement in their operation.

Otherwise this market will resolve to "No". 

The resolution source will be official U.S. federal government (e.g., Congress.gov) however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 73% for a U.S. AI safety bill—requiring provisions like prohibitions on specific AI model creation, training limits, usage restrictions, or human-in-the-loop mandates—being signed into law before 2027, driven by stalled 119th Congress progress despite over 100 AI bills introduced. Recent Senate Judiciary Committee advancement of the bipartisan GUARD Act (April 30), imposing age verification and minor bans on AI companion chatbots, falls short of qualifying criteria amid partisan divides: Democrats seek stringent safety measures, Republicans emphasize innovation and state law preemption. The Trump administration's May exploration of an executive order for pre-release AI model vetting bypasses legislation, while state patchwork (e.g., Connecticut's SB5 passage May 1) fills gaps. Approaching November midterms and historical tech regulation gridlock dim year-end enactment prospects.

This market will resolve according to the winner of the Democratic Primary for United States Senator from Michigan.

If no 2026 Michigan Democratic Senate Primary takes place, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be the first announcement of the results from the Michigan Democratic party, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting may suffice.

eventMetadata.context_description: Abdul El-Sayed leads trader consensus at 49.5% implied probability in the Michigan Democratic Senate primary, driven by his dominant support among voters under 40 (up 17 points over Mallory McMorrow) and Black Democrats per the April Emerson poll, bolstered by strong Q1 fundraising that outpaced rivals alongside recent Wayne County endorsements. McMorrow holds 28% with appeal to older voters, women, and independents, while Rep. Haley Stevens trails at 20.2% despite a narrow edge over El-Sayed in the late April Detroit News/Glengariff survey. The August 4 primary remains closely contested amid generational divides and undecideds, with no clear frontrunner above 50% in recent polling averages.

markets.question: Will inflation reach more than 5% in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Consumer Price Index (CPI) increased by greater than the listed percent over the 12 month period ending with any month in 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) reports. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index reports released for each month of 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm). Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

This market may not resolve to "No" until the December 2026 report is issued. Once the December 2026 report is issued, any revisions to previously released CPI figures will not be counted toward this market's resolution. If the CPI report for December 2026 is not issued by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve based on CPI figures which have already been made available by the BLS.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: March 2026 Consumer Price Index accelerated to 3.3% year-over-year from 2.4% in February, driven by a 12.5% energy surge—including 21.2% gasoline gains—while core CPI eased slightly to 2.6% YoY amid sticky shelter costs at 3.0%. Today's Bureau of Labor Statistics release of April CPI data, with consensus eyeing 3.7% YoY, stands as the key near-term catalyst shaping trader consensus on peak 2026 inflation. Federal Reserve's March Summary of Economic Projections targets 2.7% PCE inflation by year-end under steady policy rates, though private forecasts flag upside risks from oil shocks, supply disruptions, and tariff passthrough. June 16-17 FOMC deliberations loom as the next policy pivot point.

markets.question: Bitcoin Up or Down - May 12, 11:55AM-12:00PM ET
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Bitcoin price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the BTC/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/btc-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream BTC/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.

markets.question: Will US withdraw from NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Will US withdraw from NATO by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United States formally initiates a withdrawal from NATO or provides an official notice of denunciation to NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A notice of denunciation refers to the submission of a notice of withdrawal as per Article 13 of the North Atlantic Treaty.

Any action meeting these criteria will qualify for a “Yes” resolution regardless of if its implementation is immediately halted or delayed by judicial or other actions.

The U.S.'s exit from NATO’s integrated military command structure will not be sufficient to resolve this market to "Yes". 

The resolution source will be official information from the US government and NATO, however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's administration announced on May 2 plans to withdraw 5,000 US troops from Germany over the next 6-12 months, citing European allies' refusal to provide support—such as airspace access—during US-led operations in the Strait of Hormuz amid the ongoing Iran conflict, escalating NATO tensions. This follows April threats to exit the alliance over similar burden-sharing disputes, though a 2024 National Defense Authorization Act (Section 1250A) prohibits unilateral presidential withdrawal, mandating two-thirds Senate approval or congressional authorization. Trader consensus reflects these legal barriers and historical alliance resilience, despite rhetoric from Trump and Secretary Rubio signaling potential post-conflict reevaluation. NATO's consideration of skipping annual summits underscores strain, with Europe's accelerated defense plans as a pivotal upcoming development.

markets.question: Will Janet Horner win the 2026 Dublin-central by-election?
markets.description:
A by-election for a seat from the Dublin Central constituency in the Dáil Éireann, the lower house of the Irish parliament, is expected to take place sometime in 2026to fill the vacancy left by the resignation of Paschal Donohoe.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Dublin-Central seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

events.title: Dublin-Central By-Election Winner
events.description:
A by-election for a seat from the Dublin Central constituency in the Dáil Éireann, the lower house of the Irish parliament, is expected to take place sometime in 2026 to fill the vacancy left by the resignation of Paschal Donohoe.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the by-election for this Dublin-Central seat in the Irish Dáil Éireann.

If voting does not take place in this election or the election results are not definitively known by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting. In case of ambiguity, this market will resolve solely based on the official election results published by the Irish government, specifically the Irish parliament or “Oireachtas” (https://www.oireachtas.ie/en/elections/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Social Democrats councillor Daniel Ennis leads trader consensus at 74% implied probability to win the Dublin Central Dáil by-election on May 22, reflecting bookmaker odds favoring him as a Condorcet winner with robust first preferences and expected transfers in this three-seat, left-leaning north inner city constituency vacated by Fine Gael's Paschal Donohoe. Recent canvassing emphasizes cost-of-living pressures, housing shortages, and crime, where Ennis has gained traction, while immigration remains a minor issue per his door-to-door reports. Sinn Féin's Janice Boylan holds second at 17%, capitalizing on party leader Mary Lou McDonald's strong local base from 2020, amid coalition government tensions. Independent Gerry Hutch garners 7% wildcard curiosity despite his controversial profile, as Fine Gael's Ray McAdam lags at 5% in defending the seat.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Seoul be 22°C or higher on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Korea Meteorological Administration's latest short-term forecast, updated late May 11, projects Seoul's May 12 high near 23°C with a minimum of 15°C, but persistent cloud cover and 60% precipitation probability during afternoon peak heating hours—hourly peaks at 20–22°C under light winds and high humidity—drive trader consensus toward 20°C at 43% implied probability, closely trailed by 21°C. This aligns with May 11's observed 20°C peak at the resolving Incheon International Airport station and early-May climatological averages of 19–22°C, where reduced solar insolation from overcast skies suppresses convective warming. Ensemble models like GFS and ECMWF show uncertainty in cloud evolution; real-time observations and KMA's next hourly updates could shift odds as the day unfolds.

markets.question: Spread: Mohun Bagan Super Giant (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 26 at 10:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Mohun Bagan Super Giant" if Mohun Bagan Super Giant win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Inter Kashi FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on indiansuperleague.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Mohun Bagan Super Giant vs. Inter Kashi FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Indian Super League game, scheduled for April 26 at 10:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mohun Bagan Super Giant, sitting second in the ISL 2025-26 table with 13 points from six matches including a +11 goal difference, hold strong home form at Vivekananda Yuba Bharati Krirangan ahead of their rescheduled clash against mid-table Inter Kashi FC on May 12. The Mariners recently drew 0-0 at Bengaluru FC and sit just behind leaders Mumbai City FC, bolstered by attacking depth and midfield control driving their League Shield push. Inter Kashi, with five points from 1-2-3, struggle away, coming off a 1-2 home loss to Mumbai City and facing potential absences like Mario Barco's injury. Playoff implications loom large for the top six, with Mohun Bagan's superior head-to-head edge and recent momentum shaping trader consensus on a competitive yet lopsided matchup.

markets.question: Will the price of Bitcoin be above $80,000 on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

markets.question: Will Tarcisio de Freitas win the 2026 Brazilian presidential election?
markets.description:
A presidential election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate that wins this election.

This market includes any potential second round. If the result of this election isn't known by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., https://dadosabertos.tse.jus.br/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent polls, including Futura (May 4–8) showing President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at 38% and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro at 37% in first-round voting intentions, and Ideia (May 1–5) with Lula at 40% versus Flávio's 36%, underpin the tight trader consensus favoring Flávio slightly at 42.5% implied probability over Lula's 39.5% for Brazil's October 4 presidential election. Flávio's surge in simulated runoffs—leading 47–44 in Futura and 45–45 in Ideia—stems from consolidating right-wing support via the enduring Bolsonaro brand, despite Jair Bolsonaro's ineligibility, amid Lula's approval hovering at 44–47% with rising disapproval tied to economic pressures. Party conventions, fresh economic data, regional shifts, or candidate gaffes could widen the gap before the first round or potential October 25 runoff.

markets.question: Will Analilia Mejia win the NJ-11 Special Election by 40% or more?
markets.description:
The special election for the seat for New Jersey's 11th congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives is currently scheduled to take place on April 16, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the specified election.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher placed bracket.

If two or more candidates tie for the most valid votes in this election, and the tie is between two candidates listed, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the candidate whose listed last name comes first in alphabetical order. If the tie is between a listed candidate and an unlisted candidate, this market will resolve to the lowest margin bracket for the listed candidate. If the tie is between two or more unlisted candidates, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official sources, including https://www.nj.gov/state/.

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Final certified results from New Jersey's 11th Congressional District special election on April 16 confirm Democrat Analilia Mejia's landslide 60%-39% victory over Republican Joe Hathaway, yielding a 21-point margin that locks in trader consensus for the 20-25% outcome at overwhelming odds. The vacancy stemmed from Mikie Sherrill's resignation after winning the governorship, with Mejia prevailing in a competitive February Democratic primary before capitalizing on low-turnout dynamics favoring Democratic early and mail voting. Mejia overperformed Kamala Harris's 2024 district margin by 12 points, surging in Hispanic areas like Dover and flipping 13 precincts Harris lost, despite softer support in Jewish-heavy towns amid her progressive Israel stance. With results certified around May 4 and no outstanding ballots, revisions via recount or legal challenge remain highly improbable.

markets.question: Will Nicolae Ciucă be the next Prime Minister of Romania?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the next individual who officially assumes the office of Prime Minister of Romania by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed by the President of Romania and receive a vote of confidence from Parliament, resulting in the official formation of a new government. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister who does not receive a parliamentary vote of confidence will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister takes office by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Romania; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Romania's pro-EU coalition government under Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan collapsed on May 5, 2026, after a no-confidence vote passed with Social Democrats (PSD) joining far-right opposition amid disputes over austerity measures to address the EU's highest budget deficit. President Nicușor Dan must now consult parliamentary groups and nominate a new prime minister candidate for a confidence vote, keeping trader consensus fragmented with PSD leader Sorin Grindeanu at 24.5% implied probability due to his party's seats and prior signals of interest, ahead of PNL's Deputy Prime Minister Cătălin Predoiu at 16.5% as a reform continuity option. The tight race hinges on coalition negotiations in a hung parliament; successful PSD-PNL realignment or technocrat endorsement could separate frontrunners, while repeated failures risk snap elections before 2028.

markets.question: Will MrBeast's next video get less than 20 million views on day 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of views the next YouTube video posted by MrBeast gets in the first 24 hours after being posted. This market may not resolve until the 24 hours are complete, regardless of whether a strike is reached earlier.

If MrBeast does not post a YouTube video by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest range bracket.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market is MrBeast's YouTube channel (https://www.youtube.com/@MrBeast), specifically the 'views' counter for the described video.

Note: This market refers to MrBeast's next video to be posted. Shorts, previews, or other videos released other than the referenced video will not be considered.

markets.question: No change in Bank of Japan’s interest rates after the July 2026 meeting?
markets.description:
The Statement on Monetary Policy for the Bank of Japan's Monetary Policy meeting for July is scheduled to be released on July 31, 2026 (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm).

This market will resolve to the amount of basis points the upper bound of the short-term policy interest rate is changed by versus the level it was prior to the Bank of Japan's July 2026 meeting.

If the short-term policy interest rate is changed to a level not expressed in the displayed options, the change will be rounded up to the nearest 25 and will resolve to the relevant bracket. (e.g. if there's a cut/increase of 12.5 bps it will be considered to be 25 bps)

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official website of the Bank of Japan (https://www.boj.or.jp/en/mopo/mpmsche_minu/index.htm), however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Japan's statement for the specified meeting with relevant data is issued. If no statement is released by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Australia as the slim jury frontrunner at 26% implied probability for the Eurovision 2026 jury vote winner, driven by Delta Goodrem's polished rehearsals of "Eclipse"—a celestial pop ballad showcasing her powerhouse vocals and arena-ready staging that echoes past jury-pleasers like strong Big Five entries. France's Monroe trails closely at 21.5% with the sophisticated "Regarde!", appealing via intricate composition and live debut buzz, while Denmark's Søren Torpegaard Lund ("Før vi går hjem") at 19.5% gains from emotional ballad delivery in Melodi Grand Prix. Finland's Linda Lampenius and Pete Parkkonen's violin-fueled "Liekinheitin" (17.5%) rides Eurojury overall victory and OGAE poll momentum but lags in pure jury forecasts. Yesterday's Semi-Final 1 jury show yielded mixed feedback with no qualifiers yet confirmed; SF2 on May 14 and final jury votes post-rehearsals could swing this tight race amid historical jury-televote splits.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects National Weather Service guidance projecting a high near 85°F (29°C) in Panama City, Florida, on May 13 under partly sunny conditions with isolated sea-breeze showers, per the latest forecast update early May 11. NOAA model ensembles like GFS show minor spread amid mild high-pressure influence, with historical May averages near 86°F (30°C). Watch midday updates for refined convective outlooks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in global forecast models for Paris-Le Bourget Airport's lowest temperature on May 12, with 5°C (26%) edging 6°C (24.5%) and 4°C (19.5%) close behind, driven by an incoming northerly airflow advecting cooler continental air masses amid a broader cool spell across western Europe. Météo-France's latest guidance points to a baseline minimum near 6°C under variable cloud cover and light winds that could suppress radiative cooling, while ECMWF ensembles suggest potential drops to 4–5°C if skies partially clear overnight, allowing surface temperature inversions to deepen. Key differentiators include exact timing of post-frontal clearing and low-level stratus persistence; monitor this evening's model updates from NOAA and ECMWF for shifts before observations finalize resolution. Historical May lows at the station rarely dip below 4°C, underscoring the tight scientific balance.

events.title: What will be the #2 global Netflix show this week?
events.description:
Netflix is expected to update its global Top 10 TV shows list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, May 12, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which show this update ranks as the #2 global Netflix show.

The ranking is based on total views globally, as reported by Netflix for TV shows (English only).

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors "Should I Marry A Murderer?" as the #2 global Netflix TV show this week, with market-implied odds at 98.3%, driven by its commanding current position in Netflix's latest Top 10 rankings at #2 with 10.6 million views—trailing only "Man on Fire"'s 11 million but well ahead of #3 "Unchosen" at 8.8 million. This three-part true crime docuseries, which premiered last week, has surged on strong debut streaming metrics and word-of-mouth buzz around its gripping real-life story of pathologist Caroline Muirhead's toxic romance. As the weekly chart nears lock (typically Tuesday), final viewership tallies are unlikely to shift dramatically. Realistic upsets would require an improbable late-week surge from challengers like "Unchosen" or "Running Point" via viral social momentum, though historical patterns show new releases rarely overtake established leaders this late.

markets.question: Will Lars Boje Mathiesen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?
markets.description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Caretaker Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen leads trader consensus at 68.5% implied probability to remain Denmark's next prime minister after her Social Democrats topped the fragmented March 24, 2026, Folketing election despite seat losses, but her center-left bloc fell short of a majority in the proportional representation system. Coalition negotiations deadlocked last week when Frederiksen failed to secure a royal-mandated government, prompting King Frederik X on May 8 to task Venstre leader Troels Lund Poulsen—current Deputy Prime Minister—with forming a center-right alliance, elevating his odds to 17.1% amid potential support from Moderates' Lars Løkke Rasmussen (8.8%), a key kingmaker. Upcoming talks will test these dynamics in Denmark's multi-party parliament.

markets.question: Will Jean Castex be on the ballot for the 2027 French presidential election?
markets.description:
The next French presidential election is currently expected to be held in April 2027. Prior to the election, the French Constitutional Council is expected to publish the official list of candidates to be included on the ballot for the first round of this election.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed individual is included on the official candidate list for the first round of the 2027 French presidential election. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

If no such candidate list is published prior to the election, this market will resolve based on which candidates actually appear on the ballot when the election takes place. If no list is published and the specified election does not take place by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the official candidate list published by the French Constitutional Council; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Yoon out of custody before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if President of South Korea Yoon Suk Yeol is released from custody by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If Yoon is released but remains under house arrest, the market will still resolve to "Yes".

If Yoon is released on parole, bond, or any other condition that results in them leaving state custody, the market will resolve to "Yes".

Transporting Yoon to another location of custody (e.g., a different prison, court, or hospital within the correctional system) will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes".

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from government authorities or corrections departments; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol remains in custody following a February 2026 Seoul Central District Court life sentence for leading an insurrection tied to his December 2024 martial law declaration, upheld amid ongoing appeals. An April 29 appeals court ruling increased his separate seven-year term for obstructing arrest and bypassing cabinet processes, extending detention amid multiple charges including abuse of power. With trials continuing into mid-2026 and no bail grants or procedural releases since early detention extensions, trader consensus at 92.2% for "No" reflects judicial momentum toward prolonged incarceration, barring successful higher-court reversals or procedural breaks before 2027.

markets.question: Will Gold (GC) hit (HIGH) $5,700 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Gold (GC) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Gold (GC) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (February, April, June, August, October, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month changes automatically on the contract's First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Gold (GC) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gold futures (GC) for end-June delivery trade around $4,725 per ounce, buoyed by central banks' record Q1 2026 purchases of 244 tonnes and easing U.S. Treasury yields that enhance gold's appeal as a non-yielding safe haven. Spot prices have rallied over 5% in the past week from $4,500 levels amid softer April CPI data at 2.3% year-over-year and a weakening dollar index below 102, fueling expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts. Persistent geopolitical risks and ETF inflows underscore bullish trader sentiment, though a hawkish FOMC pivot could cap gains. Key catalysts include the June 16-17 FOMC meeting and May CPI release on June 11, with bank forecasts eyeing $5,000–$6,000 by year-end.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Frequent phone talks, including Trump's April 29 call with Putin and Putin's May 9 hint that the war is "coming to an end," signal de-escalation interest but prioritize virtual engagement over travel logistics, sanctions hurdles, and Trump's packed schedule, including an imminent Beijing summit with Xi Jinping. Putin keeps a Moscow invitation open, with a G20 Miami invite eyed for December. Shifts could arise from a durable Ukraine truce breakthrough or sudden neutral-site agreement like Turkey.

markets.question: Spread: CA Osasuna (-1.5)
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game, scheduled for May 12 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CA Osasuna" if CA Osasuna win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Club Atlético de Madrid".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: CA Osasuna vs. Club Atlético de Madrid - More Markets

eventMetadata.context_description: Atlético Madrid hold fourth place in the La Liga table with 60 points from 33 matches, firmly positioned for Champions League qualification, while Osasuna sit ninth on 42 points in mid-table security. Trader consensus likely favors the visitors due to their superior squad depth and recent 3-2 victory over Athletic Club, though that win cost midfielder Pablo Barrios a thigh injury ruling him out until mid-May, alongside defender José Giménez's similar issue. Osasuna boast a strong home record at El Sadar, with competitive head-to-head history including a 2-0 win over Atlético last season, but recent mixed form tempers upset potential. Late-season motivation and travel could influence the outcome in this pivotal fixture.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in San Francisco be 47°F or below on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the San Francisco International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the San Francisco International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/san-francisco/KSFO.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

Persistent marine layer—cool, moist stratus from Pacific onshore flow at 10-15 mph—caps peaks at 62-63°F (31% implied probability) in the leading outcome, as seen in coastal observations suppressing inland heat. However, if high pressure ridge strengthens and fog burns off earlier, allowing more solar heating, 66°F or higher (26.5%) gains traction, differentiating from 64-65°F (20.5%). Key watch: NWS evening updates and tomorrow's model runs for refined boundary layer evolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in global forecast models for Paris-Le Bourget Airport's lowest temperature on May 12, with 5°C (26%) edging 6°C (24.5%) and 4°C (19.5%) close behind, driven by an incoming northerly airflow advecting cooler continental air masses amid a broader cool spell across western Europe. Météo-France's latest guidance points to a baseline minimum near 6°C under variable cloud cover and light winds that could suppress radiative cooling, while ECMWF ensembles suggest potential drops to 4–5°C if skies partially clear overnight, allowing surface temperature inversions to deepen. Key differentiators include exact timing of post-frontal clearing and low-level stratus persistence; monitor this evening's model updates from NOAA and ECMWF for shifts before observations finalize resolution. Historical May lows at the station rarely dip below 4°C, underscoring the tight scientific balance.

markets.question: Will Trump say "Pulitzer" in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump mentions the listed term between May 1, 2026, 12:00 AM ET and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

markets.question: Mobile Legends Bang Bang: ONIC PH vs Omega Esports (BO3) - MPL Philippines Regular Season
markets.description:
This market refers to the Mobile Legends Bang Bang match between ONIC PH and Omega Esports in the MPL Philippines Regular Season, initially scheduled for May 15 at 7:30AM ET.

This market will resolve to "ONIC PH" if ONIC PH win the match against Omega Esports.

This market will resolve to "Omega Esports" if Omega Esports win the match against ONIC PH.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page. However, if https://liquipedia.net/mobilelegends/Main_Page has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid a fragile ceasefire in the US-Iran conflict centered on the Strait of Hormuz blockade, nuclear restrictions, and sanctions relief, the next diplomatic meeting remains unscheduled after initial rounds in Islamabad, Pakistan, failed to yield agreement. US delegations have been led by Vice President JD Vance alongside special envoy Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, while Iran dispatched Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliamentary Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. Iran relayed a counterproposal via Pakistani mediators on May 10, but President Trump deemed it insufficient, declaring the ceasefire "on life support" as of May 11. Regional powers including Qatar, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and Turkey coordinate backchannels, with resumption possible post-Trump-Xi summit this week and Pakistan favored as venue.

markets.question: Will Argentina’s annual inflation in 2026 be between 30% and 34.9%?
markets.description:
This is a market about Argentinian inflation over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the National Institute of Statistics and Census (INDEC) of Argentina.

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI / IPC) over the 12-month period ending in December 2026 (Variación % interanual Total nacional) according to the monthly INDEC report.

The resolution source for this market will be the INDEC Consumer Price Index report released for December 2026 (https://www.indec.gob.ar/), expected to be released in January 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by clicking on the “Precios al consumidor” option on the home page of https://www.indec.gob.ar/, and searching the pdf for the figure under “Variación % interanual Total nacional”.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly INDEC CPI (IPC) news release which reports inflation over 12 month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 33.6%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 36.3% implied probability for Argentina's 2026 annual inflation in the 30.0-34.9% range, closely tracking the Central Bank's latest REM survey median of 30.5% end-year, up 1.4 percentage points from prior estimates. This shift stems from March 2026 CPI rising 3.4% month-over-month—above consensus—pushing year-to-date accumulation to 9.4% amid seasonal education and transport pressures, despite President Milei's austerity measures curbing annual rates to 32.6% from February's 33.2%. Lower bins like 25-29.9% (24.5%) reflect optimism for further fiscal tightening, while upside risks above 40% (24.2% combined) account for potential policy slippage; April CPI data, due imminently, looms as the next catalyst.

markets.question: Will Joe Flacco start Week 1 for the Chiefs in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed player who is the starting quarterback for the Kansas City Chiefs in their Week 1 regular season game of the 2026-2027 NFL season.

If the Chiefs do not name a single player as the starting quarterback, the market will resolve based on the player who takes the most snaps at quarterback during their first offensive drive of the game. If multiple players take the same number of snaps on the Chiefs’ first offensive drive, the market will resolve to the player who took the first snap at quarterback.

This market may not resolve before the start of the Chiefs’ Week 1 regular season game. 

The resolution sources for this market will be official information from the NFL (NFL.com); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will Crude Oil (CL) hit (LOW) $40 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Crude Oil (CL) futures is equal to or below the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Crude Oil (CL) futures contracts, the active month is the nearest of the contract months listed. The active month becomes a non-active month effective two business days prior to the spot month expiration. For example; if the spot month expires on a Friday the next listed contract will be considered the Active Month on the Wednesday prior to the spot month expiration.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Crude Oil (CL) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: WTI crude oil (CL) futures for June 2026 trade near $98 per barrel, reflecting trader consensus on tightening supply amid escalating U.S.-Iran tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and OPEC+ output at a 26-year low following recent production cuts of 9.4 million barrels per day. Prices have surged over 85% year-to-date, peaking above $110 in early May before pulling back from $116 Brent-equivalent highs, driven by supply disruptions outweighing softer demand forecasts cut by 700,000 barrels per day due to regional conflict. EIA's Short-Term Energy Outlook projects a 2Q26 Brent peak at $115 before easing on rising non-OPEC+ output. Key catalysts include weekly EIA inventory reports starting May 14, potential OPEC+ policy signals, and geopolitical escalations that could push prices toward resolution thresholds by June 30.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the MD-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the MD-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. April McClain Delaney's sustained double-digit lead in recent Democratic primary polls—49% to David Trone's 38% in an April survey—bolsters trader consensus on a Democratic hold in Maryland's 6th Congressional District, rated Safe Democratic by Sabato's Crystal Ball and Solid Democratic by Cook Political Report. The district's Montgomery County Democratic suburbs offset western Maryland's GOP lean, where Delaney narrowly prevailed in her 2024 open-seat win. Weak Republican primary contenders like Robin Ficker trail in fundraising, limiting general election threats ahead of the June 23 primaries. Escalating primary rhetoric last week has not eroded the party's structural edge in this midterm cycle.

markets.question: Will SINNERS make it to the IEM Atlanta 2026 Grand Final?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for the Grand Final of IEM Atlanta 2026, scheduled to take place from May 11 to May 17, 2026.

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official ESL rules.

If the IEM Atlanta 2026 is canceled, postponed, or if the official list of IEM Atlanta 2026 Grand Final participants is not published before May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ESL website (https://pro.eslgaming.com/). However, a consensus of credible reporting (e.g., Liquipedia at https://liquipedia.net/counterstrike/Intel_Extreme_Masters/2026/Atlanta) may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: IEM Atlanta 2026 group stage opens today in double-elimination GSL format across Groups A and B, with top three teams per group advancing to single-elimination playoffs May 15–17—group winners earning semifinal byes en route to the Bo5 grand final. Vitality enters as consensus favorite atop a 27-map playoff win streak, facing new-look BC.Game roster with s1mple's debut alongside electroNic and ScrunK; NaVi eyes deep run versus Passion UA in Group B's Ukrainian derby, while recent Bucharest champion FUT clashes with NRG. Key watch: FaZe versus paiN, Liquid-Astralis, and stylistic matchups amid no major injuries or patches, as traders weigh form, map pools, and upset potential in the $1M showdown.

markets.question: Will Celtic FC vs. Dunfermline Athletic FC end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 23, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

events.title: Celtic FC vs. Dunfermline Athletic FC
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Scottish Cup game, scheduled for Saturday, May 23, 2026 between Celtic FC and Dunfermline Athletic FC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Celtic's commanding 79% implied probability stems from their Scottish Premiership pedigree and recent 3-1 Old Firm victory over Rangers on May 10, extending a strong run including wins over Hibernian and Falkirk amid a tight title race where they sit second behind Hearts. Facing Championship fourth-placers Dunfermline at neutral Hampden Park in the Scottish Cup final, traders see vast quality gap despite the Pars' giantkilling path to the decider via triumphs over higher-tier sides like Hibs. Dunfermline's 20.5% upset chance and 21% draw reflect potential from returning striker Zak Rudden and defender Freddie Turley, while Celtic manages absences like Kasper Schmeichel's shoulder issue and Tomas Cvancara's recent groin blow with squad depth.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders price near-certainty that the federal funds rate target range—currently 3.50%-3.75%—will hit 3.5% before 2027, reflecting its existing lower bound, while assigning low probabilities to sub-3% levels amid persistent inflation pressures. March 2026 CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year, the highest since mid-2024, coupled with April unemployment steady at 4.3% and nonfarm payrolls adding 115,000 jobs, signaling a resilient labor market that tempers rate-cut expectations versus the Federal Reserve's March dot plot median of low-3% by end-2027. Chair Powell's recent cautious tone underscores data dependence; today's April CPI release and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting loom as key catalysts for shifts in the market-implied policy path.

markets.question: Will France reach the 2026 FIFA World Cup final?
markets.description:
This market will resolve “Yes” if the listed team reaches the 2026 FIFA World Cup final. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed nation to advance to the 2026 FIFA World Cup final (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup final matchup has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup: Nation to Reach Final
events.description:
This market will resolve “Yes” if the listed team reaches the 2026 FIFA World Cup final. 

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed nation to advance to the 2026 FIFA World Cup final (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the associated market will resolve to "No".

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after August 2, 2026, 11:59 PM ET or the 2026 FIFA World Cup final matchup has not been declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from the FIFA; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup just one month away starting June 11, trader consensus on nations reaching the final hinges on the December 2025 group draw and paths through the expanded 24-team knockout from the round of 32, favoring Spain (Group H with Cape Verde, Saudi Arabia, Uruguay), France (Group I vs. Senegal, Iraq, Norway), and England (Group L vs. Croatia, Ghana, Panama) due to manageable group stages and opposite-bracket semifinal routes against Argentina or Brazil. Recent UEFA playoff triumphs in late March propelled Bosnia and Herzegovina, Czechia, Sweden, and Türkiye as potential dark horses, while mounting injuries—Rodrygo's ACL tear sidelining Brazil's depth, Germany's Serge Gnabry out with a torn adductor, and Spain's Lamine Yamal hamstring scare—introduce uncertainty amid final squad announcements. Host advantages boost USA (Group D), Mexico (Group A opener), and Canada (Group B), but no major developments have shifted standings in the past 30 days.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.
