The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's commanding start to the 2026 F1 season, with three wins from four grands prix including a dominant Miami victory holding off Lando Norris, has propelled him to the drivers' championship lead with 100 points and trader consensus at 37%, a 20-point edge over Mercedes teammate George Russell at 80 points and 29.5%. Mercedes' superior car pace and constructors' lead fuel the tight intra-team battle, as Antonelli's qualifying poles and adjusted race pace half-second faster than Russell in Miami underscore his rookie sensation status, though Russell's experience keeps odds competitive amid warnings of potential team tensions. McLaren's Norris (11.6%) shows upset potential with strong recent form, while Red Bull's Verstappen (6.5%) lags, heightening uncertainty ahead of the Canadian Grand Prix.

markets.question: Will the Bank of Russia make no change to the key rate after the June Meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the key rate resulting from the Bank of Russia’s June meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Russia after its June 19, 2026 meeting as listed on the official Bank of Russia calendar: https://www.cbr.ru/eng/dkp/cal_mp/#t13

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their June 19, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

events.title: Bank of Russia decision in June?
events.description:
This market will resolve according to the change in the key rate resulting from the Bank of Russia’s June meeting, relative to the level it was prior to this meeting.

The resolution source for this market is information released by the Bank of Russia after its June 19, 2026 meeting as listed on the official Bank of Russia calendar: https://www.cbr.ru/eng/dkp/cal_mp/#t13

This market may resolve as soon as the Bank of Russia’s press release for their June 19, 2026 meeting with relevant data is issued. If no decision on the key rate is issued by the end date of the next scheduled meeting, this market will resolve to the "No change" bracket.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign an 82.5% implied probability to a Bank of Russia key rate decrease in June, reflecting the central bank's April 24 cut of 50 basis points to 14.5%—its eighth consecutive easing—and baseline forecast for a 14.0–14.5% average rate through 2026 year-end, signaling further cuts amid stabilizing inflation at 5.9% YoY in March. A May 11 downgrade of Russia's 2026 GDP growth forecast to 0.4% from 1.3%, amid sanctions strain and Q1 small-business tax revenue plunging 22%, bolsters the easing case by highlighting economic deceleration. No-change odds at 16.5% capture caution over persistent underlying price pressures around 4–5% annualized, while hikes at 2.3% face barriers from absent overheating. Upcoming April CPI data could sway sentiment ahead of the June meeting.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD hovers around 1.178 as of May 11 close, rebounding from sub-1.170 lows after the Federal Reserve held the fed funds rate at 3.50%-3.75% on April 29 amid sticky inflation, contrasting the ECB's unchanged stance through its April 30 meeting. Divergent monetary policy paths—Fed pausing cuts while ECB maintains deposit rate near 2%—bolster trader sentiment for gradual euro appreciation, with consensus forecasts eyeing 1.20 by year-end on anticipated U.S. easing later. Recent March CPI at 3.3% year-over-year reinforced USD support, but eurozone data stabilization adds upside pressure. Pivotal catalysts include today's April CPI release at 8:30 AM ET and June 16-17 FOMC meeting, potentially swaying breakouts above 1.180 resistance.

markets.question: FC Cincinnati vs. Inter Miami CF: O/U 3.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming MLS game between FC Cincinnati and Inter Miami CF, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if FC Cincinnati and Inter Miami CF combine to score 4 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 4, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on mlssoccer.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Cincinnati vs. Inter Miami CF - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the MLS game, scheduled for May 13 at 7:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Inter Miami CF holds third place in the MLS Eastern Conference with 12 points from seven matches (3-1-3 record), fueled by Lionel Messi's sharp form after shaking off a February preseason hamstring strain, as confirmed in recent recovery sessions and lineups against Austin FC. FC Cincinnati, sitting mid-table after a gritty 1-1 draw at Toronto FC on April 11—where center-back Matt Miazga exited with leg discomfort—seeks to capitalize on home-field edge at TQL Stadium for the May 13 clash. Head-to-head history is evenly split (Cincinnati 5 wins, Miami 6, 3 draws in 14 MLS meetings), with Miami's attack facing Cincinnati's stout defense amid lingering concerns like Evander's foot injury recovery and potential Miazga absence. Traders eye Messi's influence, recent momentum, and injury updates as pivotal swings.

markets.question: Will the 2028 Democratic Presidential nominee be a woman?

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 67% implied probability against a woman securing the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination, driven by heavy wagering on male frontrunners like Gavin Newsom at 24% odds, amid post-2024 election introspection favoring fresh leadership over Kamala Harris despite her 29% national polling average lead as of late April. Recent Echelon Insights and Yale Youth polls from mid-April highlight shifts, with Newsom and Pete Buttigieg gaining on Harris and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, as Democrats eye governors tested in battleground states ahead of November 2026 midterms. Gretchen Whitmer's lower profile and signals against a bid further bolster male contenders in this early, wide-open primary field.

Cruz Azul's 4-2 aggregate triumph over Atlas bolsters their 25% odds amid strong +13 GD and third-place form, while today's Pachuca-Toluca clash (Pachuca up 1-0) keeps the liguilla path unpredictable in this bunched title race among regular-season elites.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former Lt. Gov. Mandela Barnes leads trader consensus at 53.5% implied probability in the crowded Wisconsin Democratic gubernatorial primary set for August 11, driven by his standout name recognition from the 2022 U.S. Senate campaign and prior statewide office, consolidating support among undecided voters who comprised over half in the February Marquette poll. markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 27°C on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Hong Kong Observatory's latest 9-day forecast, updated May 11, projects a 25–30°C range for the city's highest temperature on May 13, with mainly cloudy conditions, a few showers, and hot sunny intervals under an anticyclone aloft over southern China. This moderate heat outlook, influenced by moderating southerly winds and 70–95% humidity, drives the tightly clustered trader consensus around 24–31°C outcomes at roughly 20% implied probabilities each, reflecting inherent short-range forecast uncertainty in cloud cover and precipitation timing—more persistent sun could push toward 30°C or higher, while increased showers favor the lower 20s. Recent cooler days (23–28°C on May 11) and lingering Tropical Cyclone Hagupit east of the Philippines add variability, with daily HKO updates expected to refine model consensus before resolution based on official King's Park readings.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will G2 Esports qualify to MSI 2026?
markets.description:

This market will resolve according to which teams qualify for MSI 2026.

If the listed team officially qualifies as one of the teams invited to MSI 2026, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

Ties in standings will be broken according to the official LoL Esports rules.

If MSI 2026 is canceled, postponed indefinitely, or if the official list of MSI 2026 participants is not published before August 1, 2026, ET, this market will resolve to "No."

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Shanghai be 15°C on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects high uncertainty in Shanghai's lowest temperature on May 15, with forecasts from the China Meteorological Administration (CMA) pointing to a 20°C minimum under cloudy conditions and southerly winds below 12 km/h, while global models like those from CustomWeather and Weather Underground imply 19°C amid a slight cooling trend from recent highs near 31°C. This fragmentation across 15–23°C outcomes stems from ensemble spreads in GFS and ECMWF runs, where variable low-level cloud cover—forecasted as overcast through May 14—could suppress radiative cooling to keep lows above 20°C, or clearer skies allow drops toward 17–18°C, modulated by urban heat island effects and East China Sea sea breezes. Watch CMA updates and new model cycles over the next 48 hours for shifts ahead of resolution based on official station data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Algeria's mounting goalkeeper crisis, with Anthony Mandrea sidelined by shoulder surgery, Melvin Mastil out from hernia issues, and Luca Zidane doubtful after a jaw fracture, has eroded their defensive edge ahead of this pivotal World Cup Group J clash against Austria on June 27 in Kansas City. Both teams enter with strong recent form—Algeria unbeaten in qualifiers with solid home/away records, Austria ranked slightly higher at No. 24 versus Algeria's 28—fueling trader consensus for a razor-thin contest on neutral ground at Arrowhead Stadium. Key absences like Ramy Bensebaini's ankle injury for the Fennecs balance Austria's own concerns over Maximilian Wöber's muscular problems, while both sides eye knockout progression behind Argentina and ahead of Jordan, keeping win probabilities tightly clustered around 37% for Austria and 36% for Algeria.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Seoul be 11°C or below on May 15?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Incheon Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 15 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Incheon Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/kr/incheon/RKSI.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

FBI documents listing him as a potential co-conspirator date to 2019 investigations that yielded no case, underscoring significant evidentiary or prosecutorial barriers amid his age (88) and expired statutes for many events.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns a 90% implied probability to no change in the Reserve Bank of New Zealand's Official Cash Rate (OCR) at the upcoming May 27, 2026 Monetary Policy Statement, reflecting sticky inflation and resilient labor market dynamics that held OCR steady at 2.25% in the April decision. First-quarter 2026 CPI remained unchanged at 3.1% year-over-year—near the upper end of the 1-3% target—prompting traders to price out cuts (0.1%) while allocating 11% to potential hikes amid global inflation persistence risks flagged in the May 6 Financial Stability Report. Geopolitical tensions and domestic growth signals further support the hold, with pre-meeting surveys as the next key catalyst.

markets.question: Will SV Werder Bremen win on 2026-05-16?

If SV Werder Bremen wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

events.title: SV Werder Bremen vs. BV Borussia 09 Dortmund
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Bundesliga game, scheduled for Saturday, May 16, 2026 between SV Werder Bremen and BV Borussia 09 Dortmund.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Borussia Dortmund at 50.5% implied probability for their Bundesliga Matchday 34 visit to SV Werder Bremen, reflecting the visitors' second-place standing with 70 points and +34 goal difference after 33 matches, superior head-to-head dominance (20 wins to five), and a 3-0 home victory over Bremen in January. Bremen's 15th-place position (32 points, -21 GD) underscores their struggles, highlighted by recent losses like 1-0 at Hoffenheim and 1-3 to Augsburg, no clean sheets in six games, and key absences including Mitchell Weiser (ACL), Leonardo Bittencourt (thigh), and Keke Topp (ACL). Dortmund's mixed form (three wins, two losses in last five) and injuries to Ramy Bensebaini and Emre Can keep the matchup closely contested, with home advantage bolstering Bremen at 27% and draw at 23.5%.

markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices PL at 76% to secure the most seats among 54 contested in Brazil's October 4, 2026 Senate election, driven by its status as the largest current caucus with 15 senators and robust state-level polling favoring opposition candidates. MDB trails at 6%, leveraging centrist positioning and competitive pre-candidates in key states like São Paulo and Minas Gerais. Recent Genial/Quaest and Paraná Pesquisas surveys from late April show PL-backed figures leading in major electoral colleges amid anti-incumbent sentiment, even as presidential polls remain tight between Lula and Bolsonaro proxies. No major developments in the past week, but candidate filings and primaries could influence seat projections under state majoritarian rules.

markets.question: Ukraine agrees not to join NATO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Ukraine publicly agrees not to join NATO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An official pledge by Ukraine not to join NATO will qualify for a “Yes” resolution whether as a unilateral announcement or part of an agreement with the Russian Federation.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO for any amount of time will count (e.g. If Ukraine not to join NATO for 10 years this will qualify).

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995 “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement is an example of a qualifying agreement. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the Ukraine, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming a qualifying agreement has been reached will also count.

Any agreement or pledge made before the resolution date of this market will qualify, regardless of if/when the agreement goes into effect.

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO for any amount of time will count (e.g. If Ukraine not to join NATO for 10 years this will qualify).

An agreement by Ukraine not to join NATO as a precondition of a more comprehensive peace process or deal will qualify, even if the agreement is not finalized or part of a formalized peace deal. The September 8, 1995 “Agreed Basic Principles” between Bosnia and Yugoslavia which recognized the borders and sovereignty of Bosnia and Herzegovina, and was later formalized through the Dayton Peace Agreement is an example of a qualifying agreement. 

The primary resolution source for this market will be an official announcement by the Ukraine, however an overwhelming consensus of credible reporting confirming a qualifying agreement has been reached will also count.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 79% on Ukraine agreeing not to join NATO before 2027, reflecting stalled Russia-Ukraine peace talks where Moscow has demanded such a pledge as a precondition for de-escalation, but Kyiv has rejected concessions amid ongoing military stalemate. President Zelenskyy recently reaffirmed Ukraine's NATO aspirations, dismissing "NATO light" security guarantees and emphasizing full membership as essential post-war, while NATO allies at recent summits reiterated support without an invitation or timeline, citing unresolved conflict under alliance rules. Absent diplomatic breakthroughs—like resumed direct negotiations or U.S.-brokered deals floated in early 2026—traders see entrenched positions persisting through 2026, with EU accession talks gaining focus instead.

markets.question: Will NYC have between 4 and 5 inches of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in inches in Central Park, New York City between May 1 and May 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be NOAA, specifically the figure for May 2026 when the "Monthly summarized data" for "Central Park NY" is selected with the variable set to "Precipitation" at the https://www.weather.gov/wrh/climate?wfo=okx link once that figure is finalized for the whole month of May 2026.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 2 decimal places (e.g., 1.54), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Real Betis Balompié vs. Elche CF end in a draw?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 12, 2026
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the CO-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CO-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will gas hit (High) $4.70 by May 31?
markets.description:

Only the first two decimal digits of the reported price will be considered (e.g., if the price is reported as $3.257, this market will use $3.25 as the price).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the American Automobile Association (AAA), presently found here: https://gasprices.aaa.com/. Specifically, the cell under "Regular" and for the row "Current Avg".

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Decibel's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Decibel (https://x.com/DecibelTrade) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: US strike on Colombia by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if a US-initiated drone, missile, or air strike on the soil of Colombia is announced or credibly reported to have occurred by the listed date ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones, or missiles (including FPV and ATGM strikes as well as cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by any United States operatives, including military forces, intelligence agencies, or other U.S. government operatives, that physically impact ground territory within the listed country.

A strike on any area within the terrestrial territory (including rivers, lakes, ports, but excluding territorial sea) of the listed country counts.

Missiles or drones that are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution, regardless of whether they land territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, ground incursions, naval shelling, or cyberattacks will not qualify.

Any strike occurring during this market’s timeframe that is claimed by either Donald Trump or the U.S. government will qualify.

The primary resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

This market will remain open until the end of the second day after the resolution time. If the date/time of a qualifying strike cannot be confirmed by a consensus of credible reporting by that time, it will resolve to "No" regardless of whether a strike was later confirmed to have taken place.

markets.question: Will CZ post 0-19 posts from May 5 to May 12, 2026?
markets.description:

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

Light winds and potential clear skies post-fog clearance enable radiative cooling, though persistent low clouds or urban heat island effects could lift lows toward 50-51°F (17%). High uncertainty reflects model spread and proximity to resolution, with evening satellite imagery and 00Z model updates critical before morning observations finalize the outcome.

Malvinas Gaming (11.2%) fights from lower bracket against The New Kings tomorrow, while lower seeds like 9z Team trail after early exits. Double-elimination format keeps the race fluid amid close head-to-head history and hot streaks.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NY-21 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-21 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Elise Stefanik for her gubernatorial bid, remains a Solid Republican hold per Cook Political Report and other forecasters, driving trader consensus to 68% odds for the Republican Party nominee despite a competitive June 23 primary. Recent dueling polls—McLaughlin showing Assemblyman Robert Smullen leading Anthony Constantino 43%-33% in mid-April, countered by Constantino's February survey claiming a 27-point edge—highlight GOP infighting fueled by Trump's endorsement of Constantino over the state party's Smullen pick, yet reinforce the district's R+ partisan lean from Stefanik's landslide wins. Democrats face a fragmented five-way primary with no clear frontrunner, limiting upset potential ahead of the November 3 general election.

Barca's dominance is tempered by an injury crisis, with star winger Lamine Yamal sidelined for the season via hamstring tear, though Raphinha and Andreas Christensen recently resumed full training ahead of their May 3 Osasuna clash; doubts linger over Christensen's partial ACL recovery. markets.question: Will UK GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 1.2% and 1.5%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the UK real gross domestic product (GDP) growth rate (% change) for Q1 of 2026 compared with the same quarter a year ago in the "GDP First quarterly estimate, UK" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for May 14, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/grossdomesticproductgdp/bulletins/gdpfirstquarterlyestimateuk/previousreleases

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign an implied 48.8% probability to UK Q1 2026 GDP growth of 0.6-0.9% year-over-year, with 34.0% for 0.9-1.2%, reflecting consensus around 0.8% amid recent monthly data beats. ONS February 2026 figures showed 0.5% month-on-month GDP expansion—exceeding 0.1% economist expectations—and lifted three-month yoy growth to 0.8%, offsetting weaker January output. Bank of England’s April Monetary Policy Report pegged underlying Q1 quarterly growth at 0.2%, consistent with subdued expansion despite 3.3% March CPI inflation prompting a Bank Rate hold at 3.75%. Mixed April PMIs—manufacturing expanding to 53.7, construction contracting sharply to 39.7—signal uneven momentum, with ONS March GDP release on May 14 poised to finalize the quarter.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Liz" or "Truss" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

With threats of a leadership challenge intensifying, including a Labour MP's ultimatum to cabinet ministers by May 12, Starmer faces Kemi Badenoch, Leader of the Opposition, in the House of Commons following the State Opening of Parliament on May 13 and the King's Speech outlining the legislative agenda. Prior PMQs on April 29 highlighted defence spending critiques and economic woes, shaping expectations for Starmer's responses on NHS funding, poverty, investment priorities, and election fallout amid volatile party dynamics.

markets.question: Will Citigroup fail by June 30, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if, within the listed date range, any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open  to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: Which banks will fail by June 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed bank fails between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

For the purposes of this market, the listed bank will be considered to have “failed” if any of the following occurs under the bank’s applicable legal or regulatory framework, within the listed date range:

- The listed bank’s primary banking regulator formally declares the institution insolvent or non-viable, or withdraws or revokes the bank’s license or authorization, and such determination initiates or directly results in resolution, liquidation, wind-down, or transfer actions.
- The listed bank enters a court-ordered liquidation, statutory resolution regime, or regulator-mandated wind-down, including the use of resolution tools such as bail-ins, forced asset transfers, or the establishment of a bridge bank.
- A government or resolution authority intervenes in a manner that wipes out or subordinates existing equity of the listed bank and transfers effective control of the bank to the state or a designated resolution authority, with continued operations dependent on official intervention.
- The listed bank publicly defaults on a payment obligation, including derivatives margin, repo, or physical commodity delivery, and such default is formally acknowledged by the bank’s primary regulator or resolution authority and directly results in the initiation of resolution, liquidation, license withdrawal, or regulator-mandated transfer of the bank.
- The listed bank is subject to a compulsory merger, acquisition, or transfer of all or substantially all of its assets and liabilities ordered or directed by its primary banking regulator or resolution authority due to the bank’s financial condition or to prevent failure, regardless of whether a formal insolvency declaration or immediate equity wipeout is publicly announced at the time of transfer.

If there is a potential failure of the listed bank within this market’s date range and a qualifying regulatory or court action has occurred but has not yet been fully published by the relevant authority, this market may remain open until September 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET to allow for confirmation. If no qualifying failure is confirmed by that date, this market will resolve to “No.”

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements, filings, or actions by the listed bank’s primary banking regulator or resolution authority; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects low implied probabilities for listed banks failing by June 30, driven by the U.S. banking system's resilience post two minor 2026 failures—Metropolitan Capital Bank & Trust ($261 million assets) in January and Anchor Bank on May 1—with no subsequent closures or contagion signals. The Federal Reserve's May 2026 Financial Stability Report highlights moderate funding risks, as banks' uninsured deposit reliance remains below 2023 peaks amid stabilizing commercial real estate (CRE) pressures, despite $1.5 trillion in maturities through year-end. Regional banks face CRE loan scrutiny (average exposure ~20-30% of assets), but lower Treasury yields and revived lending support balance sheets. Key catalysts ahead: Q2 earnings in July and Dodd-Frank stress test results expected June, alongside FDIC monitoring for liquidity stress.

eventMetadata.context_description: Incumbent Rep. Mike Thompson (D) dominates trader consensus to top California's 4th Congressional District top-two primary on June 2, buoyed by 28 years in office, Democratic Party of California endorsement, and recent super PAC backing from Article One amid early voting. Challenger Eric Jones (D), a 35-year-old venture capitalist, edges out a splintered Republican field of six candidates—Sharon Brown, Mandy Ghusar, and others—for the second advancement slot, fueled by superior grassroots fundraising nearing $3.3 million from individual tech donors versus Thompson's PAC-heavy $2.9 million. Redistricting trimmed the district's D+8 partisan lean by adding conservative counties like Sutter and Yuba, but no polls show GOP consolidation; late spending surges or endorsements could shift the runner-up battle before ballots finalize.

City, fresh off a 2-1 head-to-head victory over Arsenal in April, boast Pep Guardiola's run-in expertise and Erling Haaland's scoring form despite Rodri's absence. Realistic challenges include Arsenal stumbling to drop to second or an unlikely United surge.

markets.question: Will Silver (SI) hit (HIGH) $120 by end of June?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, on any trading day, the official CME settlement price for the Active Month (front month) of Silver (SI) futures is equal to or above the listed price by the final trading day of June 2026. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

For CME Silver (SI) futures contracts, the Active Month is the nearest of CME's designated delivery-cycle months (March, May, July, September, December) that is not the spot month. The Active Month becomes a non-active month effective on its First Position Date, at which point the next eligible contract month becomes the Active Month.

Only the Active Month's official settlement price published by CME Group will be considered. Intraday trades, highs, lows, bids, offers, midpoint values, or indicative prices do not count.

Note that the settlement price may differ from the last traded price. CME's methodology to determine the settlement price can vary by commodity and contract.

Only days on which CME publishes an official settlement price for the Active Month will be included. Days without settlement prices (weekends, holidays, or market closures) are ignored.

This market will resolve based on the settlement price as it appears on the CME settlement page at the time it is first published for that trading day, regardless of any later corrections or updates.

The resolution source for this market is the CME Group website — specifically, the daily "Settlement" price for the Active Month of Silver (SI) futures.

eventMetadata.context_description: Silver futures (SI) have surged over 140% year-to-date in 2026, with front-month COMEX contracts trading near $87 per ounce as of May 12, propelled by robust industrial demand from solar photovoltaics, electric vehicles, and electronics—accounting for over 60% of total consumption—amid Silver Institute-reported supply deficits exceeding 200 million ounces annually. Recent gains accelerated last week on upbeat Chinese import data and optimism over Federal Reserve rate cuts weakening the U.S. dollar, silver's key inverse correlate. Trader sentiment reflects "wisdom of crowds" pricing in sustained deficits and green energy tailwinds, though risks include hotter inflation or Treasury yield spikes. Watch May 15 CPI data and June 10-11 FOMC meeting for policy shifts ahead of end-June resolution.

Recent developments, including a near-32°C reading on May 10 and May 11 projections of 30°C amid high-pressure dominance, have elevated sentiment above official guidance, as clear conditions maximize insolation and Beijing's urban heat island amplifies airport station readings. Key differentiators include afternoon cloud formation potentially capping at 32°C versus sustained heating to 33°C; ECMWF and GFS ensembles diverge slightly on peak boundary-layer temperatures, with real-time observations from Beijing Capital International Airport resolving the market by evening.

markets.question: Getafe CF vs. RCD Mallorca: O/U 1.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between Getafe CF and RCD Mallorca, scheduled for May 13 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Getafe CF and RCD Mallorca combine to score 2 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 2, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Getafe CF, sitting 6th in La Liga with 44 points after 33 matches, hosts relegation-threatened RCD Mallorca (17th, 35 points) on May 13 in a pivotal late-season encounter carrying Europa Conference League aspirations for the hosts and survival urgency for the visitors. Key absences shape trader consensus, including Getafe's top scorer Borja Mayoral (knee injury, long-term) and Juanmi (injury), alongside Mallorca's striker Vedat Muriqi (injury) and defender Antonio Raíllo (ankle surgery until June). Getafe boasts a narrow recent head-to-head edge (1-0 win last meeting), but Mallorca's dismal away form—one win in 16—contrasts Getafe's inconsistent home results, heightening focus on low-scoring trends and defensive matchups amid both sides' injury-hit attacks.

markets.question: Will the Starship for SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12 achieve a successful splashdown?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Starship upper stage for the twelfth Starship-SuperHeavy test flight achieves a controlled splashdown in the ocean. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

This market pertains to the twelfth Starship launch. If the rocket lifts off the pad, it will be considered the twelfth Starship launch, and this market will resolve based on it. If the rocket does not lift off the pad, it will not be considered the twelfth launch, and this market will stay open until the twelfth launch has occurred. If the twelfth launch has not occurred by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

A splashdown will be considered controlled if the Starship upper stage successfully re-enters Earth's atmosphere, remains intact during descent, and enters the water without breaking apart or exploding upon impact.

The resolution source for this market will be official video provided by SpaceX (https://www.youtube.com/c/SpaceX), as well as secondary video feeds and/or written reports if necessary.

events.title: SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12
events.description: This is a market on the outcome of the SpaceX Starship Flight Test 12.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX's Starship Flight Test 12, debuting the V3 Super Heavy booster and Ship prototypes, has gained strong trader momentum from recent hardware milestones at Starbase's Pad 2. Booster 19 nailed a full-duration 33-engine static fire on May 7, Ship 39 aced its tests earlier, and the full stack completed a successful wet dress rehearsal by May 11, loading propellants to simulated liftoff. FAA advisories open windows as early as May 12, targeting May 15, with prior licenses secured. This rapid iteration underscores SpaceX's push for reusable rocket dominance versus competitors like Blue Origin, though weather or minor anomalies could delay. Key resolution hinges on pad departure by market close.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-25 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-25 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

events.title: Will Zendaya and Tom Holland get married by December 31?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Zendaya and Tom Holland's marriage takes place by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only announcements made within this market's timeframe will qualify. No announcements made after December 31, 2026, 11:59PM ET will be considered, even if the marriage itself is announced to have taken place within the market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from Zendaya and Tom Holland, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives a 67.8% implied probability to Zendaya and Tom Holland tying the knot by December 31, driven primarily by their confirmed engagement—publicly signaled by her diamond ring debut at the 2025 Golden Globes—and intensified by early March 2026 rumors. Stylist Law Roach's SAG Actor Awards remark that "the wedding has already happened" sparked widespread speculation, amplified by Zendaya's gold band sightings at the Oscars and Paris Fashion Week, though she playfully sidestepped confirmation on Jimmy Kimmel Live!. No official announcement has materialized, underscoring the high uncertainty in celebrity personal matters, but their low-key six-year relationship and lack of denials bolster optimism for a year-end resolution amid ongoing public fascination.

markets.question: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the United Kingdom government formally designates the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

events.title: Will the UK designate the IRGC a terrorist organization by June 30?
events.description:

This includes designation as a proscribed terrorist group or organisation according to the UK Home Office, as well as the use of Royal Prerogative Powers or Orders in Council, legislation signed into law, or the creation of a new national designation that formally designates the IRGC as a terrorist organization.

Statements by government officials, including the Prime Minister, Home Office, or National Crime Service, will not count unless accompanied by a formal designation.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting or official information from the UK Government.

eventMetadata.context_description: Despite ministerial signals, including Work and Pensions Secretary Pat McFadden's May 6 statement that proscription of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organisation is "likely," the UK government has not advanced the necessary legislation by mid-May, driving trader consensus to 90.5% against designation by June 30. Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced plans in late April to introduce powers under the Crime and Policing Bill for banning state-backed groups like the IRGC—following the EU's February listing—but parliamentary debates in April yielded no passage amid procedural hurdles in the Terrorism Act 2000. With limited sessions remaining before the deadline and no Home Office confirmation, traders price in slim odds of timely enactment absent accelerated votes or executive action.

markets.question: Will Ethereum dip to $2,250 on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1 minute candle for Ethereum (ETH/USDT) on the date specified in the title, between 12:00 AM ET and 11:59 PM ET has a final "Low" price equal to or lower than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the ETH/USDT "Low" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/ETH_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" for one-minute candles selected on the top bar.

Please note that the outcome of this market depends solely on the price data from the Binance ETH/USDT trading pair. Prices from other exchanges, different trading pairs, or spot markets will not be considered for the resolution of this market.

markets.question: Will Enzo Fernández be included in Argentina's official 2026 World Cup squad list?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed player is announced as a member of the listed nation’s official squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup by June 1, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

Only full squad announcements will be considered. Prior cut lists or previous squad lists other than the officially announced squad lists will not be considered.

If a player is officially announced as part of the squad but is replaced before the nation’s first game for any reason, the corresponding market will still resolve “Yes”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after June 14, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or the listed player cannot be confirmed as a part of the listed nation’s official squad for the 2026 FIFA World Cup within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/), however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2026 FIFA World Cup kicking off in June, Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni has not yet announced the official 26-man squad for the defending champions, who topped CONMEBOL qualifiers with 38 points, driving trader focus on final call-ups amid recent injury recoveries. Key veterans like Lionel Messi, Emiliano "Dibu" Martínez, Cristian Romero, Alexis Mac Allister, and Enzo Fernández anchor predictions, blended with rising stars such as Nico Paz, Franco Mastantuono, and Giuliano Simeone per Panini album leaks and media previews. April setbacks for Lautaro Martínez, Martínez, and Leandro Paredes raised concerns, but positive updates and March friendly inclusions have stabilized sentiment; upcoming warm-ups against Algeria and others will clarify fitness and Scaloni's loyalty to proven performers versus youth integration.

markets.question: Will Cristiano Ronaldo announce his retirement in 2026?
markets.description:
This polymarket will resolve to "Yes" if Cristiano Ronaldo officially announces his retirement from club soccer by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

To resolve "Yes", the announcement must clearly state that Ronaldo is retiring from club-level professional soccer. The announcement must come from Ronaldo himself or his official representatives.

Retirement from international soccer alone will not qualify. 

The retirement must be intended to take effect immediately or prior to the start of the next club season following the announcement.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official statements from Cristiano Ronaldo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Cristiano Ronaldo's sustained elite form in the Saudi Pro League, where he continues scoring regularly for Al-Nassr—topping the table with 79 points from 31 matches as of late April 2026—and his new contract extension through June 2027 underpin traders' strong consensus at 88% implied probability against a 2026 retirement announcement. The 41-year-old Portugal captain, managed for fitness ahead of the 2026 World Cup (confirmed as his final major international tournament last November), has added goals like his 969th career tally in AFC Champions League Two play, chasing 1,000 while showing no injury concerns or performance dips. Prior statements emphasize retiring post-World Cup or upon reaching milestones, with no recent developments signaling an earlier exit.

markets.question: Will Finland win on 2026-05-31?
markets.description:

If Finland wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Germany vs. Finland
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA International Friendlies game, scheduled for Sunday, May 31, 2026 between Germany and Finland.

eventMetadata.context_description: Germany enters the May 31 international friendly in Mainz as overwhelming trader favorites at 85% implied probability, driven by their superior FIFA ranking, home advantage at MEWA Arena, and recent dominant form including Florian Wirtz's masterclass—two goals and two assists—in a thrilling victory over Switzerland six weeks ago. Head-to-head history favors Die Mannschaft, unbeaten against Finland with a 2-0 win in their last 2016 friendly, underscoring the quality gap. Despite Serge Gnabry's long-term muscle tear ruling him out for the 2025-26 season and recovery concerns for Jamal Musiala and Kai Havertz, Germany's squad depth maintains edge. Finland, hampered by Lucas Bergström's injury and lacking firepower, sees draw and win at 10% each, reflecting limited upset potential absent major German absences.

markets.question: Will ByteDance have the highest IPO Market Cap 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the company that achieves the highest market capitalization in U.S. dollars based on the official closing price on its first trading day in 2026.

This market will resolve to a company that completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

Market capitalization is defined as the total number of outstanding shares multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If two or more companies have exactly equal highest closing market capitalizations, this market will resolve to the company whose listed name comes first alphabetically.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In case the respective company's primary exchange’s official listing page does not report in U.S. dollars, it will be converted to U.S. dollars using the U.S. Federal Reserve Board’s H.10 foreign exchange reference rate for the relevant currency pair on the company’s first trading day (https://www.federalreserve.gov/releases/h10/). If no such rate is available for the relevant trading day, the most recent previously published rate will be used. If the relevant currency is not listed, another credible exchange rate source will be used.

A listed company may resolve to "No" as soon as it becomes unable to complete an IPO, including due to acquisition, merger, or absorption by an entity that is already publicly traded.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on the respective companies’ first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: SpaceX dominates trader consensus at 87.5% implied probability for the largest 2026 IPO by market cap, driven by its April 1 confidential S-1 filing with the SEC, followed by an April 6 banker meeting outlining a blockbuster $75 billion raise targeting $1.75 trillion valuation—potentially the biggest ever. The February merger absorbing xAI at a $250 billion valuation integrates advanced artificial intelligence into Starlink's satellite network and rocket operations, boosting projected 2026 revenue to $20 billion while retaining Elon Musk's control via dual-class shares; a June roadshow and late-May prospectus loom as key catalysts. xAI lingers at 25.5% amid merger overhang but subordinate scale. Anthropic's Q4 IPO talks and explosive revenue growth trail due to smaller ~$60 billion ambitions, while OpenAI's May signals of a 2027 delay despite an $852 billion funding round erode its 2.9% odds, underscoring SpaceX's unmatched momentum in space tech and AI convergence.

markets.question: Hurupay FDV above $30M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Hurupay's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Hurupay (https://x.com/HurupayApp) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Guangzhou be 26°C or below on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Guangzhou Baiyun International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/guangzhou/ZGGG.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a razor-thin lead for 32°C at 26% implied probability over 31°C at 25%, reflecting inherent uncertainty in short-range temperature forecasts amid Guangzhou's humid subtropical conditions. The China Meteorological Administration's latest guidance calls for thunderstorms on May 12 with a 30°C high—slightly trailing at 17%—following recent observed peaks of 30-31°C on May 10-11 despite partial cloudiness and showers. Key differentiators include timing of convective onset from an advancing frontal system, variable cloud cover suppressing or allowing urban heat island effects, and ensemble model spreads of 1-2°C across ECMWF and GFS runs. Updated CMA bulletins and Baiyun Airport observations expected later today could sharpen the outlook before resolution based on official station data.

markets.question: Will Seoul have less than 100mm of precipitation in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total precipitation in mm in Seoul in May, 2026, according to the Korea Meteorological Administration's information for the region/branch of "Seoul".

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source for this market will be the Korea Meteorological Administration, specifically the figure for monthly precipitation in mm when the classification is set for "ground", the region/branch is set for "Seoul", the element is set for "precipitation", and the period is set for the month of May at the https://data.kma.go.kr/climate/RankState/selectRankStatisticsDivisionList.do link once the figure is finalized for the whole month of May 2026.

If the relevant data is not available by July 7, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, another credible resolution source will be chosen.

The resolution source for this market measures precipitation to 1 decimal place (e.g., 1.5), thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. Any revisions to precipitation recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.description:

This market will resolve as soon as the listed price is hit, or once the value for the specified end date is finalized. If not all relevant data has been finalized by the end of the 14th calendar date afterward (ET), this market will resolve based on the data available at that time.

Revisions made to previously published data points, before all relevant data points have been finalized, will be considered; however, they will not disqualify a previously published data point from resolving this market. Revisions made after all relevant data points have been finalized will not be considered.

Vast.ai's median stands at $3.50/hour (p25-p75: $2.72-$4.95), with Ornn aggregating near $3.85 amid hyperscaler utilization above 95% and neocloud capacity constraints; low availability under 40 instances underscores tightening dynamics defying hardware depreciation. eventMetadata.context_description: Recent University of Houston Hobby School polling released May 5 shows Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton leading incumbent Sen. John Cornyn 48%-45% among likely Republican primary runoff voters, with 7% undecided, driving trader consensus to price Paxton as the favorite ahead of the May 26 contest. This narrow edge, within the +/-2.8% margin of error, builds on Paxton's earlier 8-point TPOR lead in mid-April and reflects his strong appeal to the GOP base critical of Cornyn's Senate leadership role amid mixed prior surveys favoring the incumbent. Both advanced from the fragmented March 3 primary without a majority; Cornyn holds a fundraising advantage with over $8 million cash-on-hand, but Paxton's momentum positions him as the implied winner in market pricing, though the race remains closely contested.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' closely contested implied probabilities—clustered tightly around the 2.1%–2.9% bins for South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation—reflect April 2026's year-over-year headline rate accelerating to 2.6%, a 21-month high driven by surging petroleum prices amid Middle East tensions, while core inflation held steady at 2.2%. This uptick from March's 2.2% has edged sentiment toward the higher 2.7%–2.9% outcome at 33.3%, though Bank of Korea projections of mid-to-high 2% averages, IMF's 2.5% forecast, and stable underlying pressures keep lower bins competitive at 29.9%–32.1%. Key swing factors include May CPI data and the BOK's late-May policy meeting, where persistent energy costs could prompt a hawkish pivot from the 2.50% base rate.
