eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors Michael Carrick at 85.5% implied probability to become Manchester United's next permanent manager, driven by his transformative interim stint since Ruben Amorim's January sacking. Carrick has steered the Red Devils to Champions League qualification, amassing the Premier League's most points in that span with wins over rivals like Manchester City, Arsenal, Chelsea, and Liverpool, restoring calm and tactical clarity. INEOS decision-makers have reportedly given unanimous green light, discarding rivals like Kieran McKenna and Thomas Tuchel while planning his backroom with Bayern's Aaron Danks. Recent delays in formal confirmation, amid calls for swift clarity to aid summer transfers, represent the main hurdle, though his club familiarity and momentum make an upset unlikely absent a dramatic reversal.

markets.question: Will the 2026 trade deficit be between 900B and 1T?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026, as reported by the US Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) and the US Census Bureau (USCB) in the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026, expected to be released in February 2027.

Upon publication, the specified release will be made available at: https://www.bea.gov/news/current-releases

The relevant figure may be found in the annual summary under “Exports, Imports, and Balance (exhibit 1)”. Changes in the BEA or USCB’s reporting format will not disqualify a relevant published figure from counting.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 from the US Bureau of Economic Analysis and the US Census Bureau. If this release is not published by April 30, 2027 ET, another credible source on the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 will be chosen.

Note: any revisions to the annual US Goods and Services Deficit for 2026 made after the publication of the “U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services” release for December and Annual 2026 will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: The US trade deficit widened to $60.3 billion in March per Bureau of Economic Analysis data released early May, as imports surged 2.3% to $381.2 billion while exports rose a milder 2%, pushing the goods deficit to $88.7 billion despite services surplus gains. This recent import momentum amid resilient consumer demand keeps trader consensus tightly split between 800–900B (34.5%) and 900B–1T (31.0%) for full-year 2026, reflecting annualized paces near $720–850B so far but expectations of widening. President Trump's tariff push, including a 10% universal levy ruled illegal by the Court of International Trade on May 7, adds volatility—successful appeals or new executive actions could curb imports and favor lower bins, while litigation delays or shipping slowdowns might sustain higher deficits. April BEA data, due soon, represents the next major catalyst.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment in the race for the top AI model by December 31 hinges on the intense competition among closed-source frontrunners OpenAI, Google DeepMind, and Anthropic, whose large language models dominate benchmarks like the LMSYS Chatbot Arena and Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index. OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Instant, released May 5, solidified its edge with 52.5% fewer hallucinations and superior reasoning, briefly reclaiming the lead from Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview and Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.7. Chinese open-weight challengers like Moonshot's Kimi K2.6 and DeepSeek V4 Pro are narrowing the gap to within 6 points on intelligence metrics but trail on agentic coding and hard reasoning tasks. Recent U.S. government pre-release review pacts with Google, xAI, and others signal heightened regulatory scrutiny, while anticipated frontier model drops from all major labs before year-end could swing leaderboard positions amid rapid iteration cycles.

markets.question: Games Total: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Invictus Gaming and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 12 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Over" if Invictus Gaming and Bilibili Gaming play 3 or more games in this series.

If fewer than 3 games are played, this market will resolve to "Under".

Games won by forfeit, disqualification, walkover, or default are counted towards the total, provided that the match is completed.

If the match is canceled (not played at all for any reason), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's match forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends due to the clinching game being forfeited this will count as a completed match.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: Invictus Gaming vs Bilibili Gaming (BO3) - LPL Group Ascend
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Invictus Gaming and Bilibili Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, initially scheduled for May 12 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Invictus Gaming" if Invictus Gaming win the match against Bilibili Gaming.

This market will resolve to "Bilibili Gaming" if Bilibili Gaming win the match against Invictus Gaming.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: Bilibili Gaming tops LPL Group Ascend standings at 9-2 with an 18-7 map differential, fresh off a 2-0 sweep of JD Gaming, fueling trader consensus on their favoritism against mid-table Invictus Gaming (5-7, 13-16 maps). IG gained momentum with recent 2-0 wins over Ninjas in Pyjamas and Team WE, but their poor 20% win rate over the past month and 1-2 loss to BLG three weeks ago underscore vulnerabilities. BLG holds a commanding head-to-head edge, unbeaten against IG since August 2025 across five BO3s, with Knight's midlane dominance a key factor in this double round-robin clash critical for playoff seeding. No roster changes or injury concerns reported ahead of the May 12 BO3.

markets.question: Will União Brasil (UNIÃO) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:
The next federal Senate election is scheduled to take place in Brazil on October 4, 2026, in which two-thirds of the Senate’s 81 seats will be contested.

This market will resolve according to the political party that wins the greatest number of seats in the next Brazilian Senate election. 

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known definitively by June 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the result of the election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the Brazilian government, specifically the Superior Electoral Court (Tribunal Superior Eleitoral, TSE) (e.g., www.tse.jus.br/eleicoes/resultados-eleicoes).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices PL at 76% to secure the most seats among 54 contested in Brazil's October 4, 2026 Senate election, driven by its status as the largest current caucus with 15 senators and robust state-level polling favoring opposition candidates. MDB trails at 6%, leveraging centrist positioning and competitive pre-candidates in key states like São Paulo and Minas Gerais. Recent Genial/Quaest and Paraná Pesquisas surveys from late April show PL-backed figures leading in major electoral colleges amid anti-incumbent sentiment, even as presidential polls remain tight between Lula and Bolsonaro proxies. No major developments in the past week, but candidate filings and primaries could influence seat projections under state majoritarian rules.

markets.question: Will "Swapped" be the #2 global Netflix movie this week?
markets.description:
Netflix is expected to update its global Top 10 TV movies list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, May 12, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which movie this update ranks as the #2 global Netflix movie.

The ranking is based on total views globally, as reported by Netflix for Global Top 10 Movies (English only).

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly backs Apex as the #2 global Netflix movie this week at 99% implied probability, driven by its commanding position in the latest daily streaming metrics on aggregator sites like FlixPatrol, where it trails new release Swapped (866 points) but leads challengers My Dearest Assassin (603) and Remarkably Bright Creatures (602) by a safe margin. The Charlize Theron-led survival thriller, now in its third week, benefits from sustained word-of-mouth, strong international holdover views across 80+ countries, and star power that propelled it to #1 in prior weeks with over 40 million views. An upset would require a dramatic late-week surge from close competitors like Remarkably Bright Creatures via viral social buzz or regional spikes, though final weekly tallies locking Monday favor Apex's momentum.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Chicago be between 50-51°F on May 13?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 13 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Chicago O'Hare Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/il/chicago/KORD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in National Weather Service and global model forecasts (GFS, ECMWF) projecting Chicago's May 13 high temperature around 60-64°F at O'Hare Airport, well below the mid-May climatological normal of 70°F. A lingering upper-level trough over the Great Lakes has delivered cool air advection and cloudy conditions over the past week, suppressing highs amid variable spring patterns including recent temperature drops. Differentiating factors include model spread on afternoon cloud breaks, boundary layer mixing heights, and Lake Michigan breeze strength, which could boost peaks toward 64°F or cap them nearer 60°F. New 12z model runs and morning soundings today will refine probabilities ahead of resolution.

markets.question: Tshisekedi out as President of the DRC by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if Félix Tshisekedi ceases to be President of the Democratic Republic of the Congo for any period of time between market creation and the specified date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

An announcement of Félix Tshisekedi's resignation/removal before this market's end date will immediately resolve this market to "Yes", regardless of when the announced resignation/removal goes into effect.

If the specified individual is detained, effectively removed from the specified position, or otherwise permanently prevented from fulfilling the duties of the specified position within this market’s timeframe, it will qualify for a “Yes” resolution.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Félix Tshisekedi and the government of the Democratic Republic of the Congo; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Félix Tshisekedi remains securely in office midway through his second term, which extends to 2028 under DRC constitutional limits allowing two five-year mandates, driving trader consensus to a 91.5% implied probability against his removal by year-end 2026. In a May 6 press conference, Tshisekedi signaled openness to a third term if public support materializes and warned that ongoing eastern conflict with M23 rebels could delay 2028 elections, prompting opposition outcry over potential constitutional amendments but no immediate challenges to his leadership. Recent actions, including U.S.-endorsed sanctions on rival Joseph Kabila and mining revenue audits, underscore his control amid rebel tensions, with no coups, impeachments, or resignations reported; late-breaking military escalation or health issues could still shift odds.

markets.question: Will Club Bolívar win on 2026-05-19?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for May 19, 2026
If Club Bolívar wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Fluminense FC vs. Club Bolívar
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Copa Libertadores game, scheduled for Tuesday, May 19, 2026 between Fluminense FC and Club Bolívar.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fluminense commands 74% trader consensus for victory in their Copa Libertadores Group C home match against Club Bolívar at the Maracanã, reflecting the venue's decisive edge after the Tricolor's 0-2 first-leg loss in La Paz's extreme high altitude, where five key absences—including Germán Cano (muscle), Luciano Acosta (knee), and Nonato—hampered their efforts. Bolívar thrives domestically with recent wins like 6-0 over Real Tomayapo but historically falters away at sea level, pricing them at 13.5% with draw at 19.5% amid upset potential. Fluminense's recent Brasileirão draw versus Vitória, met with fan boos, intensifies pressure on coach Luis Zubeldía as the bottom-placed side eyes advancement, bolstered by a packed schedule and club-driven full-stadium initiative.
